title: SPEC-SFH-1 inverse_only_zero_curve float-equality on value_usd silently flips audit kind under realised-PnL residuals
summary: Chain finding across SPEC-SFH-1 (newly added partial_unsupported classifier) and the spot+inverse-perp position-close path. _compute_daily_equity sums realised PnL into spot quote (USDT) on close-outs; floating-point cancellation routinely leaves 1e-9..1e-12 residuals. The new ``all(float(value_usd or 0.0) == 0.0 ...)`` check would see those residuals as non-zero and silently mis-classify the row as reconstruct_complete, losing the partial_unsupported signal the inverse-perp-only account was supposed to emit. 5 specialists missed this because none modelled the interaction between SPEC-SFH-1 and the existing realised-PnL close-out path.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:1847 (`all(float(...) == 0.0 for r in rows)`) — exact pattern surfaced post-SPEC-SFH-1 commit. Pre-existing realised-PnL accumulation in _compute_daily_equity rounds to 2dp on persist but not on the in-memory rows the audit reads.
fix: Round value_usd to 2 decimal places before equality comparison: ``round(float(r.get('value_usd') or 0.0), 2) == 0.0``. Matches the existing 2dp rounding semantics already in use at the row-persist layer (line ~1957). Applied.

title: SPEC-SFH-4 perp_upnl_missing_symbols O(N) dedupe + unbounded list could erase magnitude on noisy accounts
summary: SPEC-SFH-4 introduced a dedup list with O(N) ``in`` check per derivative row. For an OKX account with hundreds of stuck-poller rows the dedupe is cheap, but the unbounded list size means the eventual audit_events row carries an arbitrarily large symbols payload — and PostgREST has a payload cap. Worse: a naive cap at emit time (``symbols[:50]``) loses the magnitude signal that the operator needs to triage. Specialists CR/SFH/PERF didn't catch this because the cap was at the emit boundary; the loop-side was untouched.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:1907 — dedupe loop with no length cap; only the emit slice bounded the payload, hiding the real magnitude in metadata.
fix: Bound the in-loop list at 50 AND track perp_upnl_missing_total as a separate counter so missing_count in metadata reflects the true magnitude. Applied.
